title: Route collapses Spotify failures into a successful idle response
reasoning: The handler returns the same 200 JSON body for three materially different states: no configured access token, a real idle playback state, and any exception while talking to Spotify. That makes downstream consumers of the now-playing widget unable to distinguish 'nothing is playing' from 'the integration is broken or Spotify is unavailable', so outages will silently present as valid idle data.
recommendation: Preserve the idle payload only for the intentional 'no token / not playing' cases, and return a non-2xx status or an explicit error field when token retrieval or Spotify fetch fails unexpectedly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this route, so there is nothing asserting how transport or auth failures should be represented.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add route tests that mock `getSpotifyAccessToken` and `fetchNowPlaying` to verify: 200 idle for missing token, 200 payload for active playback, and a non-2xx or explicit error response when Spotify calls throw.
